verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 encodes two transcript variants with distinct fates: the longer transcript produces a canonical trans-Golgi type II TM protein in most cells/tissues. The shorter transcript produces a form that undergoes proteolytic cleavage in the stem region, releasing a soluble catalytic domain. This processed/cleaved form is documented to function as a 'cell surface form' and recognition molecule during cell-cell and cell-matrix interactions, specifically in lactating mammary tissue as part of the lactose synthase complex. The surface form is restricted to this specialized tissue/developmental context (lactation) rather than baseline. The protein body does reach the cell surface, but only in the narrow lactating-mammary-lineage state.
